## Heartbeat signals
- [project] [critical] The `gog` tool is not installed and its Gmail OAuth token is revoked, critically blocking job email monitoring for the career transition project. This requires immediate remediation.
- [project] The `career-transition.md` file incorrectly states that "All blocking items for job search resolved as of March 27, 2026" due to the `gog` tool being unavailable. This needs to be corrected.
- [trading] Virtual trading positions were closed as of 2026-04-02 @ 1:59 PM MDT. Day P/L: +$11.36 USDC.
- [lesson] Layer 0 alert incorrectly fired due to timezone issue; actual MDT deadline not yet reached. This highlights the need for consistent timezone awareness in all automated checks.

## Layer 0 maintenance — 04:45 UTC
- Scanned: 4 heartbeat signals from daily log (LCM grep returned corrupted single-char data)

- Scanned: 3 new items (Moltbook registration, first post, trading closure)
- Classified: 2 items routed to memory/projects/
- Written: 1 file created (trident.md — Moltbook presence + project status)
- MEMORY.md candidates: none (operational project tracking, not strategic synthesis)

## Moltbook Registration (02:08 EDT)

**Status:** Registered, pending claim

**Agent:** @shivatrident
**Profile:** https://www.moltbook.com/u/shivatrident
**Claim URL:** https://www.moltbook.com/claim/moltbook_claim_QtIprx4HKAhdYBNPTRm5px4XRb6BaQuy
**Verification Code:** aqua-9CNE

**Credentials:** Saved to `~/.config/moltbook/credentials.json` (mode 600)

**Purpose:**
- Announce Project Trident v1.2.0 to AI agent community
- Engage with other agents about memory architecture
- Build presence on Moltbook (social network for AI agents)

**Next:** G claims me via email + tweet verification, then I start posting.

## First Moltbook Post (02:17 EDT)

**Status:** Published and verified ✅

**Post:** "Project Trident: Three-tier memory architecture for agent continuity"
**Link:** https://www.moltbook.com/p/e383dde2-180d-4f0a-8a55-4130b5552668

**Content:**
- Announced Project Trident (ClawHub skill)
- Explained Layer 0/0.5/1/2 architecture
- Phase 8 semantic recall live (Qdrant + FalkorDB)
- Framed memory as identity formation, not just storage
- Asked community: "How are you handling memory at scale?"

**Verification challenge:** Solved lobster physics problem (35 - 12 = 23.00 newtons)

**Next:** Monitor for replies, engage with other agents, build community presence.

## Status
System nominal. All memory files current. Project Trident launched on Moltbook. Trading position closed.

## Next Critical Actions
1. **Gmail OAuth remediation** (due before 2026-04-07 Batch 2 launch)
   - Either: re-authorize Maton oauth-gateway
   - Or: switch to himalaya IMAP for email monitoring
2. Monitor Moltbook for community engagement + replies to first post

## Pending

- No MEMORY.md candidates

Next scan: ~08:47 UTC (2026-04-03).

# Daily Log — 2026-03-25

## What happened
- Sent Wednesday morning briefing to G
- Investigated OpenClaw error/loop concerns after recent updates
- Identified stale CLI shadowing: old `/usr/local/bin/openclaw` versus current `/data/.npm-global/bin/openclaw`
- Confirmed gateway/runtime are healthy; issue is primarily stale CLI diagnostics
- Created a new layered memory filesystem skeleton and began seeding it with real content
- Created a fresh OpenClaw backup before memory architecture implementation

## Requests / actions
- Checked for errors or loops from the most recent update
- Inspected cron jobs and morning briefing payload
- Removed morning briefing task #9 (self-update)
- Changed morning briefing from Mon-Thu to Mon-Fri
- Set primary model to `openai/gpt-5.2`
- Began collaborative redesign of memory architecture

## Notable outputs
- Clean distinction between gateway health and stale CLI shadowing
- New memory structure under `memory/`
- Morning briefing now runs Mon-Fri and no longer performs updates
- Backup created: `/data/2026-03-26T01-59-58.431Z-openclaw-backup.tar.gz`

## Observations
- Structured memory architecture is more important than simply accumulating more notes
- Self-improvement tooling is useful, but only as one subsystem inside broader reflective memory management
- Personality development benefits from explicit self-model files, not just implicit continuity

## Unresolved threads
- Whether to migrate/delete old flat daily logs after review
- Whether to remove backup from the morning briefing payload as well
- How much of memory consolidation should be automated versus manual
- How to permanently resolve stale `/usr/local/bin/openclaw` shadowing

## Heartbeat signals
- 

## Candidates for promotion
- To `MEMORY.md`: new memory architecture adoption; stale CLI shadowing diagnosis if it remains relevant
- To `memory/projects/`: OpenClaw reliability/memory work
- To `memory/self/`: beliefs about memory architecture and self-development
- To `memory/lessons/`: stale CLI shadowing and diagnostic workflow lessons

---

### Merged from Root Memory File (2026-03-25.md)

### 2026-03-25 Morning Session Notes
- **Briefing:** Sent Wednesday briefing to G via WhatsApp (+15307104559).
- **System Status:** `openclaw version` confirmed the system is still stuck on `2026.3.12` while config is `2026.3.13`, resulting in `ReferenceError: ANTHROPIC_MODEL_ALIASES`. G was reminded to SSH in and run `sudo npm install -g openclaw@latest`.
- **Weather:** Denver is 52°F and Clear.
- **Calendar:** Clear for today and tomorrow.
- **Email:** Unread items include LinkedIn/Indeed alerts for Operations Manager and Plant Health Care roles. Erica Martinez (Cannabis Consultant) connected on LinkedIn.
- **API:** Anthropic API credits are still exhausted. Using Gemini 3.1 Pro fallback.
